audit_meta.note: Third run after PR #12 closed the 7 residual partials. Compare to 2026-05-29T034026-Z (first) and 2026-05-29T050007-Z (re-audit).

evidence: No hardcoded secrets (grep: 0). Server uses only key-less public APIs.
.gitignore now present and ignores .env / .env.* (added PR #1)
RESOLVED (PR #12): .env.example added; gitleaks secret-scan workflow (.github/workflows/security.yml) runs on push/PR; .gitignore covers .env*. No secrets in code.
No print() in src/ (grep: 0)
structlog now configured to render to sys.stderr only; stdout stays the MCP channel (logging_config.py, PR #7)
Verified at runtime: GET /healthz logs to stderr, stdout clean
follow_redirects=False on the shared client (api_client.py, PR #1) — closes redirect-based SSRF
Code-layer egress allow-list assert_host_allowed() before every request; all hosts https (api_client.py, PR #2)
k8s NetworkPolicy blocks private + link-local ranges incl. 169.254.0.0/16 (deploy/kubernetes.yaml, PR #9)
No tool accepts a user-supplied URL — all hosts are fixed constants/registry
No custom session handling; HTTP session IDs delegated to FastMCP
auth_model=none and data is public read-only → no per-user session to bind; session hijacking has no privilege impact
RESOLVED (PR #12): README 'Sessions & Authentication' (DE/EN) documents the unauthenticated-by-design model and the user_id binding requirement for any future authenticated deployment.
No 0.0.0.0 default in code; FastMCP default host 127.0.0.1
0.0.0.0 is set ONLY via the container env SWISSTOPO_HTTP_HOST (Dockerfile, PR #9)
docs/deployment.md documents local vs container binding
All 13 tools read-only; no write/send capability
Lethal-Trifecta assessment table documented in README/README.de 'Security & Compliance' (PR #3): <=1 of 3 legs
No os.system / shell=True / eval / exec / os.popen / subprocess (grep: 0)
No filesystem path construction from user input
Tool handlers are transport-agnostic (only typed params; no request object)
Server supports stdio + streamable-http
RESOLVED (PR #12): config.py provides a pydantic-settings Settings object (host/port/origins/log level); server.py drives transport/logging from it instead of ad-hoc argv/environ.
13 use-case-oriented tools across 6 API families; anchor query in ~2-3 calls
All 13 tools declare explicit, behavior-consistent annotations (server.py)
Execution errors are caught and returned as a structured ToolResponse with is_error=true (models.py, PR #8) instead of raising
Error paths covered by tests
RESOLVED (PR #12): error contract documented (DE/EN) — execution errors -> ToolResponse.is_error; protocol errors -> SDK JSON-RPC codes (-32602/-32601); protocol-error tests added.
handle_api_error masks unexpected exceptions ('Unerwarteter interner Fehler') and never returns traceback/str(e) (api_client.py, PR #3)
Intentional ValueError/PermissionError messages preserved; originals logged to stderr via structlog (PR #7)
gaps: FastMCP from the mcp SDK has no mask_error_details constructor flag; equivalent masking is implemented in-handler instead

evidence: 311 tests; mocked unit tests + @pytest.mark.live markers registered; CI runs 'pytest -m not live'
New regression suites: shared client, egress, input validation, http app, logging, responses
gaps: No separate scheduled nightly live-test workflow

evidence: Phase 1 (read-only) declared in README 'Security & Compliance' and docs/roadmap.md (PR #3)
All tools read-only — consistent with Phase 1
k8s manifest defaults to replicas: 1 (per-pod sessions safe) (PR #10)
Sticky-session example provided (HAProxy header-stick / NGINX cookie affinity / shared store) in deploy/ingress-sticky-sessions.yaml
docs/deployment.md 'Scaling out' documents the requirement before raising replicas
FastMCP lifespan creates one shared httpx.AsyncClient reused across all calls (server.py + api_client.py, PR #1)
Regression test test_shared_client.py verifies reuse + non-closing
build_http_app configures CORSMiddleware with expose_headers=['Mcp-Session-Id'] and explicit (non-wildcard) origins (server.py, PR #3)
test_http_app.py verifies the config
follow_redirects=False removes the rebinding/redirect vector (PR #1)
Hosts are a fixed code-layer allow-list (frozenset) — no attacker-controlled hostname to rebind (PR #2)
k8s egress NetworkPolicy blocks private/link-local ranges (PR #9)
gaps: Explicit DNS resolve-once/IP-pinning is not implemented; unnecessary here because no user-controlled hostnames are fetched (documented rationale)

evidence: Default transport is stdio (mcp.run()); HTTP only via explicit --http
README documents local stdio primarily + separate cloud/container section (docs/deployment.md)
Multi-stage Dockerfile runs as non-root UID 10001 with HEALTHCHECK (PR #9)
k8s securityContext: runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, capabilities.drop ALL, seccomp RuntimeDefault, tmpfs /tmp (deploy/kubernetes.yaml)
docs/deployment.md maps each control to SEC-007
Public Open Data; only non-secret env var (SWISSTOPO_OEREB_CANTONS); no API keys needed
All input models use Pydantic strict=True + extra='forbid' (PR #3)
Whitelist pattern constraints on free-text/id/coords/lang/canton/egrid fields; numeric ge/le bounds
test_input_validation.py covers rejection of control chars / path traversal / injection-style input
Code-layer ALLOWED_HOSTS frozenset + assert_host_allowed() before each request (api_client.py, PR #2)
Network-layer egress NetworkPolicy (deploy/kubernetes.yaml, PR #9)
docs/network-egress.md documents hosts + update procedure; test_egress_allowlist.py guards drift
Consistent snake_case swisstopo_ tool names; no special chars
Every tool has a meaningful one-sentence description; server-level instructions give cross-tool guidance
RESOLVED (PR #12): all 13 tool descriptions expanded with <use_case>/<important_notes> tags, differentiating the similar feature-query tools.
Empty results now carry match_type='none' in the ToolResponse envelope (PR #8)
Geocoding empty result returns an actionable hint (refine query / use origins) (PR #3)
Most tools return thought-complete results; elevation_profile aggregates points
RESOLVED (PR #12): README 'Tool workflows' (DE/EN) documents the discovery chains; each tool description names its next step.
README 'MCP Primitives' section documents and justifies the tools-only design (PR #3)
All mandatory files/dirs present; src-layout; README.de parity; modular tools; CHANGELOG Keep-a-Changelog
mcp[cli] pinned to the 1.x major in pyproject.toml (PR #3)
.github/dependabot.yml proposes monthly SDK/action updates
README 'MCP Protocol Version' section + CHANGELOG document the policy
gaps: protocolVersion is not pinned as a literal in code because the mcp SDK negotiates it; mitigated via the SDK major-pin

evidence: Every ToolResponse carries source + license fields (swisstopo OGD; cantonal OEREB terms) (models.py, PR #8)
README documents OGD data source/licence
structlog dependency; JSON to stderr; configure_logging() idempotent (logging_config.py, PR #7)
All 13 handlers wrapped with log_tool_call: tool_invoked/completed/failed + bound tool name, correlation_id, duration; upstream requests + masked errors logged
test_logging.py covers the levels and bound context
README has anchor demo, ASCII architecture, limits, bilingual parity, CONTRIBUTING, CHANGELOG
Added Security & Compliance, Response Format, deployment docs (PRs #3/#8/#9)
All 13 tools return a structured ToolResponse (results/count/match_type + source/license/provenance/retrieved_at + summary) (PR #8)
FastMCP emits structured content + output schema; verified in test_responses.py
Most tools are single fast upstream calls where progress reporting is unnecessary
RESOLVED (PR #12): Context injected into elevation_profile and get_oereb_extract with ctx.info / ctx.report_progress (optional param; test_context.py covers it).
No pre/postinstall hooks; standard entry point; README shows full install command
publish.yml uses OIDC Trusted Publisher (Sigstore) signing
